message.content:
You are confirmer #2, independent. Confirm this fix is BOTH appropriate (correct, no regression, idiomatic) AND complete (the named test DISCRIMINATES — fails if reverted). Read the actual changed file + test at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay.
Fix CV-M-1 in src/assay_engine/methodology/confirm.py
What: stability_threshold confirm-time default is a None sentinel (was 0.9); locked-wins-else-supplied, fall back to 0.9 only when neither given — a locked threshold !=0.9 is honored without restating (fixes a follow-on regression of H-001).
Test(s): test_locked_stability_threshold_is_honored_without_restating_it (+ existing override-raises test still passes)
Verdict CONFIRMED or CONCERN; be specific.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

input.command: cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && git diff HEAD~1 -- src/assay_engine/methodology/confirm.py tests/test_confirm.py
input.description: Show the confirm.py and test diff

content.content:
diff --git a/src/assay_engine/methodology/confirm.py b/src/assay_engine/methodology/confirm.py
index dbd5ff9..bfe6d87 100644
--- a/src/assay_engine/methodology/confirm.py
+++ b/src/assay_engine/methodology/confirm.py
@@ -333,7 +333,7 @@ def confirm_whole_corpus(
     null_distribution: Sequence[float],
     alpha: float,
     resample_statistics: Sequence[float] | None = None,
-    stability_threshold: float = 0.9,
+    stability_threshold: float | None = None,
     predicted_direction: Direction | None = None,
     authority: TimestampAuthority | None = None,
 ) -> Verdict:
@@ -360,12 +360,15 @@ def confirm_whole_corpus(
     # Cross-check the confirm-time thresholds against any pre-registered ones (#H-001): a locked
     # alpha / stability bar cannot be overridden post-hoc.
     alpha = cast(float, _resolve_locked_param(hypothesis.alpha, alpha, "alpha"))
-    stability_threshold = cast(
-        float,
-        _resolve_locked_param(
-            hypothesis.stability_threshold, stability_threshold, "stability_threshold"
-        ),
+    # stability_threshold's confirm-time default is None (a sentinel), NOT 0.9 (#CV-M-1): a fixed
+    # 0.9 default was indistinguishable from a caller explicitly passing 0.9, so a hypothesis that
+    # locked a threshold != 0.9 ALWAYS raised a spurious "contradicts pre-registered" error unless
+    # the caller restated it. Resolve locked-wins-else-supplied, then fall back to 0.9 only when
+    # neither was given.
+    resolved_stability = _resolve_locked_param(
+        hypothesis.stability_threshold, stability_threshold, "stability_threshold"
     )
+    stability_threshold = 0.9 if resolved_stability is None else resolved_stability
     _validate_alpha(alpha)
     _validate_finite("observed", observed)
     if not null_distribution:
diff --git a/tests/test_confirm.py b/tests/test_confirm.py
index 8ff3b43..a985953 100644
--- a/tests/test_confirm.py
+++ b/tests/test_confirm.py
@@ -271,6 +271,25 @@ def test_locked_alpha_cannot_be_overridden_at_confirm_time():
     assert v.label is VerdictLabel.SUPPORTED
 
 
+def test_locked_stability_threshold_is_honored_without_restating_it():
+    # #CV-M-1: a hypothesis that locked stability_threshold != 0.9 must be confirmable WITHOUT the
+    # caller restating it. The confirm-time default is a None sentinel (not 0.9), so the locked
+    # value is used rather than spuriously "contradicting" the 0.9 default.
+    h = _locked(HypothesisKind.WHOLE_CORPUS, predicted_direction="greater")
+    object.__setattr__(h, "stability_threshold", 0.6)  # locked a non-default bar
+    # 60% of resamples reproduce the effect → below 0.9 but meets the locked 0.6
+    resamples = [100.0] * 6 + [-100.0] * 4
+    v = confirm_whole_corpus(
+        h,
+        observed=10.0,
+        null_distribution=[0.0] * 100,
+        alpha=0.05,
+        resample_statistics=resamples,
+    )  # NOTE: stability_threshold NOT passed — the locked 0.6 must govern, not the 0.9 default
+    assert v.evidence["stability_threshold"] == 0.6
+    assert v.label is VerdictLabel.SUPPORTED  # 0.6 stability >= locked 0.6 bar
+
+
 def test_locked_stability_threshold_cannot_be_overridden_at_confirm_time():
     # #H-001: the stability bar is decision-bearing too — a pre-registered stability_threshold
     # cannot be lowered post-hoc to flip indeterminate->supported.

content.text: Now let me look at `_resolve_locked_param` to understand the semantics.

content.content:
207:    alpha = cast(float, _resolve_locked_param(hypothesis.alpha, alpha, "alpha"))  # #H-001
270:def _resolve_locked_param(locked: float | None, supplied: float | None, name: str) -> float | None:
362:    alpha = cast(float, _resolve_locked_param(hypothesis.alpha, alpha, "alpha"))
368:    resolved_stability = _resolve_locked_param(

270	def _resolve_locked_param(locked: float | None, supplied: float | None, name: str) -> float | None:
271	    """Cross-check a confirm-time threshold against the pre-registered one (pass 5, #H-001).
272	
273	    If the hypothesis locked the value, the confirm-time argument may only AGREE with it (a
274	    mismatch raises) — the analyst cannot pick alpha / the stability bar after seeing the data
275	    (threshold HARKing). The locked value wins. If nothing was locked, the supplied value stands
276	    (exploratory / opted-out use), mirroring how direction worked before it could be locked.
277	    """
278	    if locked is not None:
279	        if supplied is not None and supplied != locked:
280	            raise ValueError(
281	                f"{name} argument {supplied!r} contradicts the hypothesis's pre-registered "
282	                f"{name} {locked!r} — the threshold is fixed at lock time (no post-hoc choice)"
283	            )
284	        return locked
285	    return supplied
286	
287	
288	_VALID_DIRECTIONS = ("greater", "less")
289	
290	
291	def _resolve_direction(hypothesis: Hypothesis, predicted_direction: Direction | None) -> Direction:
292	    """Direction is fixed at lock time on the hypothesis (issue #24); the confirm-time argument is
293	    optional and, if given, must MATCH the pre-registered one — it can cross-check, never supply.
294	
295	    A hypothesis reaching here has already passed the confirm gate, so it is locked and its
296	    ``predicted_direction`` is part of the cryptographic binding. A ``None`` locked direction
297	    therefore means the tail was *not* pre-registered; accepting a confirm-time direction in that
298	    case would let the analyst pick the tail AFTER seeing the data (HARKing), flipping
299	    supported↔contradicted on a hypothesis whose lock attests "no direction" (audit pass 4,
300	    #G-001). The tail must be committed before confirmation, so a directionless locked hypothesis
301	    is refused outright rather than completed with a post-hoc direction.
302

339	) -> Verdict:
340	    """Confirm a whole-corpus hypothesis against a null distribution + measured stability.
341	
342	    Pass ``authority`` to verify pre-registration in full (content binding + timestamp +
343	    lock-before-now); omit it to fall back to the presence gate (see :func:`require_locked`).
344	
345	    The predicted tail is taken from the hypothesis's pre-registered ``predicted_direction``
346	    (issue #24). The pattern must (a) beat the null at ``alpha`` in the predicted tail and
347	    (b) be **stable across resamples** — measured by the engine from ``resample_statistics``,
348	    not asserted by the caller (audit pass 2, issue #21). Outcomes:
349	
350	    - significant in the predicted tail AND stable → ``supported``;
351	    - significant in the *opposite* tail → ``contradicted``;
352	    - unstable, not significant, or stability not assessed → ``indeterminate``.
353	
354	    If ``resample_statistics`` is omitted, stability cannot be measured, so ``supported`` is
355	    unavailable and the verdict is ``indeterminate`` ("stability not assessed").
356	    """
357	    if hypothesis.kind is not HypothesisKind.WHOLE_CORPUS:
358	        raise ValueError("confirm_whole_corpus requires a WHOLE_CORPUS hypothesis")
359	    _gate_preregistration(hypothesis, authority)
360	    # Cross-check the confirm-time thresholds against any pre-registered ones (#H-001): a locked
361	    # alpha / stability bar cannot be overridden post-hoc.
362	    alpha = cast(float, _resolve_locked_param(hypothesis.alpha, alpha, "alpha"))
363	    # stability_threshold's confirm-time default is None (a sentinel), NOT 0.9 (#CV-M-1): a fixed
364	    # 0.9 default was indistinguishable from a caller explicitly passing 0.9, so a hypothesis that
365	    # locked a threshold != 0.9 ALWAYS raised a spurious "contradicts pre-registered" error unless
366	    # the caller restated it. Resolve locked-wins-else-supplied, then fall back to 0.9 only when
367	    # neither was given.
368	    resolved_stability = _resolve_locked_param(
369	        hypothesis.stability_threshold, stability_threshold, "stability_threshold"
370	    )
371	    stability_threshold = 0.9 if resolved_stability is None else resolved_stability
372	    _validate_alpha(alpha)

def _resolve_locked_param(locked: float | None, supplied: float | None, name: str) -> float | None:
        """Cross-check a confirm-time threshold against the pre-registered one (pass 5, #H-001).
    
        If the hypothesis locked the value, the confirm-time argument may only AGREE with it (a
        mismatch raises) — the analyst cannot pick alpha / the stability bar after seeing the data
        (threshold HARKing). The locked value wins. If nothing was locked, the supplied value stands
        (exploratory / opted-out use), mirroring how direction worked before it could be locked.
        """
        if locked is not None:
            if supplied is not None and supplied != locked:
>               raise ValueError(
                    f"{name} argument {supplied!r} contradicts the hypothesis's pre-registered "
                    f"{name} {locked!r} — the threshold is fixed at lock time (no post-hoc choice)"
                )
E               ValueError: stability_threshold argument 0.9 contradicts the hypothesis's pre-registered stability_threshold 0.6 — the threshold is fixed at lock time (no post-hoc choice)

content.text:
The file is restored to the fixed version (44 tests pass, no diff in the confirm.py stat output). My discrimination check is complete.

Assessment:
- The fix changes the `stability_threshold` parameter default from `0.9` to `None` (sentinel), then resolves locked-wins-else-supplied via the existing `_resolve_locked_param` (which already returns `float | None`), falling back to `0.9` only when neither locked nor supplied.
- This correctly fixes the H-001 follow-on regression: previously the literal `0.9` default was passed as `supplied`, so a hypothesis locking any threshold != 0.9 always raised the spurious "contradicts pre-registered" ValueError unless the caller restated it.
- The override-raises test (`test_locked_stability_threshold_cannot_be_overridden_at_confirm_time`) still passes — explicit conflicting values still raise, because `None` sentinel only suppresses the default, not real caller input.
- Idiomatic: mirrors the alpha handling pattern; `_resolve_locked_param`'s existing `float | None` return type is now used properly (the old `cast(float, ...)` was masking the sentinel issue).
- Discrimination verified: reverting both the signature default and the resolution block to the old form makes the new test fail with exactly the spurious ValueError, while the override test stays green.

input.reasoning:
Fix in /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/methodology/confirm.py is correct, regression-free, and idiomatic. It changes confirm_whole_corpus's stability_threshold default from 0.9 to a None sentinel, resolves locked-wins-else-supplied via the existing _resolve_locked_param (which already returns float|None), and falls back to 0.9 only when neither locked nor supplied. This fixes the H-001 follow-on regression where the literal 0.9 default was passed as the "supplied" arg, so any hypothesis locking a threshold != 0.9 always raised a spurious "contradicts pre-registered" ValueError unless the caller restated it. The change mirrors the existing alpha-handling pattern and finally uses _resolve_locked_param's float|None return type properly (the old cast(float, ...) masked the sentinel problem).

Discrimination verified by reversion: I reverted both the signature default (back to float = 0.9) and the resolution block (back to cast(float, _resolve_locked_param(...))). With the old code, test_locked_stability_threshold_is_honored_without_restating_it FAILS with exactly "stability_threshold argument 0.9 contradicts the hypothesis's pre-registered stability_threshold 0.6", while the override-raises test still passes (1 failed, 1 passed). Restoring the fix: both targeted tests pass and the full tests/test_confirm.py passes (44 passed). The named test therefore genuinely discriminates and the existing override-raises guard is preserved.
